<!--
Thanks for contributing to ContractorAI!
Please read CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/gitflow/README.md before opening a PR:
  - CONTRIBUTING.md        — contribution workflow and conventions
  - docs/gitflow/README.md — branching model (GitFlow), target branches, releases
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? Keep it short and concrete. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply. These map to Conventional Commit types used in PR titles. -->

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `style` — formatting, no code behavior change
- [ ] `refactor` — code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — adding or correcting tests
- [ ] `build` — build system or external dependencies
- [ ] `ci` — CI/CD configuration
- [ ] `chore` — maintenance, tooling, other
- [ ] `revert` — reverts a previous change

## Related issues

<!-- Link related issues, e.g. "Closes #123" or "Relates to #456". -->

## How was this tested?

<!-- Describe the tests you ran and how to reproduce them (unit tests, manual steps, etc.). -->

## Screenshots (if UI)

<!-- Before/after screenshots or a short recording for any user-facing UI change. Delete this section if not applicable. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] PR title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) (e.g. `feat(app): add project dashboard`)
- [ ] Target branch is correct per GitFlow: work branches → `develop`; `release/*` and `hotfix/*` → `main`
- [ ] `npm run test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run typecheck` pass locally
- [ ] Documentation updated where needed (docs/, README, code comments)
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or private data committed
